source.next_step: Try one last re-engage: SE-led architecture workshop; if no response, move to churn cadence.
recent_activity: 2026-02-20 - Inbound demo request via website; AE assigned (Ethan).
2026-02-25 - Discovery call with Head of ML + Product. Confirmed primary workload: document search + reranking for research desk.
2026-03-01 - Live demo (Zoom, Fireflies id: FF-923844). Attended: CTO, Head of ML, 2 SREs. Demo focused on latency and dedicated routing.
2026-03-02 - Demo follow-up email with pricing deck and short POC plan (sent).
2026-03-03 - Security questionnaire shared (basic SOC2 + infra diagram requested).
2026-03-04 - Two reminder emails and a voicemail; no reply from CTO/Head of ML.
2026-03-05 - SE (Priya) attempted architectural follow-up Slack message to their engineer; channel left unread.
source.notes:
Quick summary: strong technical interest after demo — liked dedicated routing and caching features. CTO said: "latency profiles look great but we need SSO + audit log answers before legal." Asked for architecture diagram and SOC2 evidence. After materials sent (pricing + security FAQ) they went quiet. Possible reasons: internal budget freeze for Q2; primary ML lead on parental leave (no champion). Competitors in play: OpenAI (for hosted simplicity) and Pinecone for vector infra. POC not scheduled.

Sales chatter / next steps (fragments):
- "Try to re-open via 1-pager that maps to their security answers (Ethan)."
- "Offer a 2-week dedicated pilot with limited data residency and audit logs as proof — needs legal OK. (Priya)"
- "If no response by 2026-03-20 -> mark as stalled and transition to nurture sequence."

Stalled tickbox: post-demo no follow-up; likely closed-lost if no re-engagement by forecast month.

source.next_step: POC kickoff: confirm test harness and telemetry contract; finalize rate-limit SLA
blockers: legal reviewing T&Cs for tokenized billing
concerns about per-user rate limits causing editor jitter
source.notes:
Overview: Arcadia Promptcraft builds a VS Code extension that provides streaming completions, inline suggestions and fuzzy signature help for JS/TS + Python. Team is engineering-first; prototype shipped but performance issues at scale.

Primary ask: low-latency streaming + per-extension per-user rate limits. They want median inline suggestion RTT <80ms for local dev workflows and tail P99 <200ms for short completions (<64 tokens). Want to support model/version pinning per-channel so customers can choose a lightweight quantized variant for IDE while defaulting to higher-quality model on demand.

Security: Must integrate with customer SSO (SAML), audit logs for suggestion events, and KMS integration for secrets. Data residency not strict but they want VPC option for enterprise customers.

Metrics/POC success criteria: 1) median suggestion latency <80ms; 2) acceptance rate uplift >25% vs baseline; 3) cost per active dev under target ($X/day, discussed in pricing deck); 4) no regressions in suggestion quality measured by curated eval prompts.

Quote from CTO on call: "If we can't guarantee smooth inline experience at scale, customers churn — latency is everything."

Notes from demo: stream looks good on 5-10 dev scale; on 500+ DAU they saw buffering and token drops. Discussed batching/caching and per-session KV cache to keep prefix latencies low. SE presented alternate: route short inline completions to a quantized code model with aggressive KV caching, fallback to larger model for multi-line completions.

Operational asks: per-extension quotas, dynamic rate limits by org tier, token-level cost telemetry in console, hooks to disable streaming for specific users/orgs.

Proposed next steps: get security packet completed, share POC test harness (drive link), align on SLOs and rate-limit config, sign POC SOW (6 weeks).
timeline: 2025-11-05 - First inbound: product team demo (self-serve sign-up)
2025-11-18 - AE intro call (Jordan B.) — documented latency targets and use cases
2025-12-10 - Security questionnaire submitted (initial), awaiting addendum
2026-01-08 - Architect deep-dive with SE (Priya K.) — discussed KV cache + prefix caching approach
2026-02-12 - Demo + live coding session (Fireflies ff_2026-02-12_8672) — streaming OK at small scale
2026-02-26 - Follow-up: pricing deck shared, requested per-user quota examples (drive link)
2026-03-01 - Legal & procurement flagged tokenized billing questions; AE advised to prepare alternate contract language
2026-03-05 - Pre-POC alignment; set POC kickoff target for 2026-03-15 pending security signoff

source.next_step: Mark stalled; one final outreach email with concise ROI appendix and security rebuttal; re-touch in 30d
source.notes:
- Demo attended 2026-02-18: product, eng manager, infra lead present.
- Showed live hosted API flow, routing fallback, and Dedicated latency numbers.
- Ask: low-latency reranking for product pages (p95 < 120ms) + embeddings for faceted search.
- Security was clear on residency + KMS; infra said must run in VPC or private offering; interested in dedicated pool.
- AE asked for evaluation dataset & expected QPS; PG said they'd share but dataset never arrived.
- After demo: multiple follow-up emails + 2 voicemails, no reply.
- 2026-02-25: SE sent security FAQ + sample KMS integration notes (see Drive link).
- 2026-03-01: procurement mentioned vendor shortlist; AE heard rumor they favored GoliathAI on price + existing relationship.
- Quote from Product Head on demo: "very promising but we need legal + security to sign off — timeline unclear."
- Current status: unresponsive post-demo. Likely internal reprioritization or champion moved.
- Suggested next moves: one concise value email (ROI and risk mitigation), include estimated cost delta vs GoliathAI, offer 2-week pilot on Dedicated with strict data controls.
- Risk flags: no champion, procurement bias, security still has unanswered questions around key management and residency timing.
- Notes cadence: AE attempted 2/20, 2/24, 3/03; no replies.
- Sales comment: keep in follow-up cadence but deprioritize active pursuit until re-engage or until next product release that maps to their ask.

source.industry: Customer support software / AI agent platform
source.next_step: Run a 7-day hosted API POC with streaming chat on FAST tier + capture TTFT/p95/p99 and cost deltas vs current provider; in parallel, Redwood to propose Dedicated VPC sizing + override/precedence plan for their peak hours.
blockers: Needs clearer guarantees around streaming tail latency (p99) and time-to-first-token under burst
Security review: asks for retention controls + audit log export details (they have regulated customers)
Wants a single 'fast default' that is safe without tuning (concerned about batching foot-guns)
recent_activity_timeline: 2026-01-29: Inbound via website demo request. Notes: 'agent streaming latency is our #1 KPI; customers feel it immediately.'

2026-02-03: Demo w/ Ava Chen + Ethan Park. Walked through tiered batching (fast/standard/cost-efficient) and how it affects queueing + batching. Customer asked for 'fast tier default for all streaming routes'.

2026-02-12: POC check-in (Fireflies). Shared early canary learnings on fast-tier p99 protection and reason codes ('why this profile'). They agreed to provide real prompt-length histogram + concurrency profile.
2026-02-18: Dedicated discussion. Asked about per-tenant overrides + precedence (customer override > route policy > tier default).
2026-02-26: Technical follow-up: clarified that tier selection changes runtime behavior only (batching/admission/backpressure), not logging/retention of prompt data.
source.record_body:
Account summary (Northlake Customer Support AI)

Firmographics / context
- Mid-market support platform building AI agent add-on for live chat + email. They sell to SaaS + fintech; have a few customers that demand tighter audit + retention controls.
- Stack today: mix of vendor LLM APIs + some self-hosted experimentation. They want to standardize on one inference platform and stop hand-tuning batching.
- Deployment: prefers VPC for production (customer expectations + data isolation). Open to starting hosted API for POC.

Primary use case
- Support-agent chat (streaming) with tool calling.
  - High sensitivity to time-to-first-token (TTFT) and streaming smoothness (chunk cadence).
  - They explicitly said: "If the agent pauses for 2–3 seconds mid-stream, our users think it broke." 
- Secondary: summarization of long tickets, embeddings + reranking for internal KB retrieval.

Requirements (captured across discovery + demo)
- Latency
  - Streaming endpoints are the critical path. They asked for a clear 'FAST' tier that they can set-and-forget.
  - Targets they mentioned (not formally committed yet): TTFT < ~350–500ms p95 (region-dependent), and p99 "no multi-second stalls" during decode.
  - Concern: under bursts, they see tail latency spikes with other providers (queue depth grows; token streaming becomes bursty).
- Traffic shape
  - Concurrency: normal 50–120 concurrent chats; peak events 300–600 concurrent (product launches / incident storms).
  - Prompt lengths: mix of short chat turns + occasional long context (ticket history + KB snippets). They admitted their worst-case prompts are "ugly" (copy/paste logs).
  - Mix: streaming chat dominates daytime; summarization batch jobs off-hours.
- Cost
  - Cost sensitive but not at the expense of perceived responsiveness. They like the idea of cost-efficient tier for async summarization, but want guardrails.
  - Asked whether tier defaults can be applied per-route (streaming chat fast; summarization cost-efficient).
- Model preferences
  - They’re model-agnostic but asked about strong 8B/70B class options for agent chat; they want fallback behavior if a model family is constrained.
  - They care about output stability during rollouts; asked how Redwood detects regressions when changing batching defaults.
- Controls / ergonomics
  - Key request: "Fast as the default for streaming." They do not want to tune max_batch_tokens / wait_ms knobs directly.
  - Wants visibility into the effective profile + why it was chosen (override vs default vs overload mode). Reason codes resonated.
  - Wants an easy override mechanism for enterprise: route-level policy in Console, plus a header/SDK param for experiments.

Security / compliance notes
- Asked for: SSO/SAML for Console, audit logs (who changed policies/tiers), retention controls, and KMS integration for any stored config/telemetry.
- Confirmed that tier selection should not change data handling/logging; they were sensitive to "does fast tier log more?" Answer: no; runtime behavior only.

Buying / stakeholders
- Champion: Head of Product (agent experience) + platform lead (infra). Procurement will care about VPC + SLA.
- They want a concrete rollout story: start hosted, then Dedicated VPC once metrics prove out.

Open questions / follow-ups
- Need their real workload stats: prompt-length distribution (prefill tokens), response length distribution (decode tokens), and streaming on/off split per route.
- Need to align on which SLO tier maps to which endpoints (fast for streaming agent chat; standard for internal tools; cost-efficient for async summarization).
- They asked for guidance on overload behavior per tier (shed load vs increase batching). We should propose a default overload policy for their agent route and show what happens at peak.

Risks
- Mixed workloads on same capacity: they may run streaming chat and summarization concurrently; if they do, we need clear route separation or policy precedence to avoid chat p99 getting dragged.
- Tail latency expectations are aggressive; we should set expectations with published tier definitions and highlight that fast-tier protects tail via tighter wait budgets + admission control.

Next steps (agreed)
1) Northlake sends: 7-day traffic shape snapshot (tokens in/out, prompt buckets, concurrency over time, streaming ratio).
2) Redwood (Ethan Park) provides: POC plan + how to force FAST tier for streaming endpoints (include SDK/header example) and how to monitor TTFT + p99.
3) Redwood to propose Dedicated VPC sizing + override/precedence approach for peak-hour protection.
4) Schedule security checklist review (SSO/SAML, audit log export, retention controls) once POC metrics are positive.

source.next_step: Re-open when security review completes or when budget unlocked; AE to try one last executive nudge (email + LinkedIn) week of 2026-03-15
blockers: security review stalled (CISO requested SOC2 + KMS proof)
timeline: 2026-01-15 - Lead created via trade show follow-up (insurtech summit)
2026-01-22 - Intro call (AE/Ethan) - product fit looked strong for policy search
2026-02-05 - SE scoping call (Priya) - discussed latency targets and on-prem options
2026-02-12 - Pricing sent (drive link) and POC proposal shared
2026-02-18 - Live demo attended by product + CISO; demo recording link in Fireflies
2026-02-25 - Planned follow-up (AE) — customer said they'd run internal security review
2026-03-02 - AE sent 3rd follow-up after missed 2/25 sync; voicemail left, no reply
2026-03-05 - AE flagged as stalled in CRM; recommended pause until security sign-off
source.notes:
Attended demo 2026-02-18 — positive reaction to routing/fallback and prefix caching. CISO attended and specifically asked for:
- SOC2 Type II evidence and a KMS/HSM integration diagram.
- Data residency guarantee for EU policies (they have EU customers).
Customer said they'd run a security review and revert; we scheduled a 2/25 sync that was cancelled by them.
Post-demo engagement: 2 follow-up emails (pricing + POC template), one voicemail. No answer.
Signals from contacts: product lead liked cost/latency profile. CISO cautious — "we can't go forward without KMS and retention controls in the contract."
Sales notes: possible loss scenarios = budget freeze (finance paused new vendor spend for Q2) or selection of competitor with stronger enterprise pack (they were demoing Anthropic enterprise as well).
AE actions taken: shared SOC2 link, KMS architecture doc, and redwood security FAQ (drive). Offered a short security deep-dive with infra team (SE + solutions architect).
Current status: cold. Recommended status = nurture; revisit after Q2 budget review or if security team asks for technical deep-dive.
Next tangible: AE to send one exec-level email week of 2026-03-15 asking for decision or permission to close as lost.

title: Lucid Harbor Analytics
preview:
Attended demo 2026-02-18 — positive reaction to routing/fallback and prefix caching. CISO attended and specifically asked for:
- SOC2 Type II evidence and a KMS/HSM integration diagram.
- Data residency guarantee for EU policies (they have EU customers).
Customer said they'd run a security review and revert; we scheduled a 2/25 sync that was cancelled by them.
Post-demo engagement: 2 follow-up emails (pricing + POC template), one voicemail. No answer.
Signals from contacts: product lead liked cost/latency profile. CISO cautious — "we can't go forward without KMS and retention controls in the contract."
Sales notes: possible loss scenarios = budget freeze (finance paused new vendor spend for Q2) or selection of competitor with stronger enterprise pack (they were demoing Anthropic enterprise as well).
AE actions taken: shared SOC2 link, KMS architecture doc, and redwood security FAQ (drive). Offered a short security deep-dive with infra team (SE + solutions architect).
Current status: cold. Recommended status = nurture; revisit after Q2 budget review or if security team asks for technical deep-dive.
Next tangible: AE to send one exec-level email week of 2026-03-15 asking for decision or permission to close as lost.

source.next_step: AE to re-engage in two weeks; remind PO and security reviewer; offer short pilot credit and security Q&A session
source.notes:
Demo completed 2026-02-18. Attendees: Head of Product (Maya Lin), Eng Manager (Tom Alvarez), Security lead (Rebecca Ho), AE Jordan Blake, SE Priya Natarajan. They were enthusiastic about latency and the reranking demo (specifically preferring model variants for cost control). Key quote from Maya: 'Sharp latency and predictable cost are table stakes — we need ironclad security sign-off before pilots.' Security asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS integration diagram, and an answer about audit log retention. We provided SOC2 summary and KMS architecture (drive links above) during the demo.

Post-demo status: two follow-up emails and one Loom sent (02/19, 02/24, 03/03). No reply from Maya or Rebecca. AE left voicemail 03/05; LinkedIn message 03/09. SE offered a 2-week pilot with a dedicated SE hours (no-charge) to unblock eval; haven’t heard back. Prod team appears to have paused new pilots due to budget freeze (internal FYQ re-plan) per a short note from procurement on 02/27. No clear internal champion identified — Maya is stretched and ownership appears to have shifted to a new head of platform (not in attendee list).

Technical requirements recap: latency p95 target <150ms for chat/rerank, peak traffic ~120 QPS for search queries, embeddings throughput for nightly indexing. Model preference: open model with ability to route to cheaper quantized variants for rerank; fallbacks to smaller models during peak. Must support VPC deployment or dedicated private endpoint; require SSO/SAML, KMS for key management, and 1 year audit log retention. Cost sensitivity: high — wants clear unit economics and suggestions on batching/quantization.

Risk assessment: stalled-to-cold. High risk of closed-lost unless a new champion surfaces or procurement budget re-opens. Competitors actively engaged (we saw a follow-up from OpenAI reps on 02/26 per Maya's note).

Action items so far: delivered pricing deck and technical annex (drive links), scheduled security deep-dive offer (SE available week of 03/15), proposed 2-week pilot credit. Recommendation: preserve as warm but deprioritized — attempt one more high-value touch (security office hours + tailored pilot T&Cs) then move to nurture drip (quarterly check-ins).
timeline: 2025-11-18 - Initial lead captured via events signup (Webinar: AI for Commerce)
2025-12-03 - Intro discovery call, scoped use cases and volume estimates
2026-01-12 - Technical scoping with SE (Priya). Confirmed VPC requirement and SSO needs
2026-02-04 - Commercial intro; pricing expectations discussed
2026-02-18 - Live demo (attended by Product, Eng, Security). Ran rerank and chat latency scenarios
2026-02-19 - Sent demo recap + SOC2 and KMS diagrams (drive links)
2026-02-24 - AE follow-up with proposed pilot terms and SOW draft
2026-02-27 - Procurement mentions company-wide hiring/budget freeze (short note)
2026-03-03 - SE sent Loom deep-dive on batching/quantization; offered pilot credit
2026-03-05 - AE left voicemail; no response
2026-03-09 - AE LinkedIn outreach; no response
2026-03-15 - Proposed security office hours (pending reply)

source.next_step: Customer to validate streaming stability via eu-west with proxy buffering disabled; Redwood to share best-practice streaming client settings
blockers: Intermittent streaming stall in eu-west when routed through corporate proxy
source.notes:
- 2026-02-10: Customer reported streaming sometimes hangs mid-response (connection stays open; no terminal [DONE]). Logged as SUP-1911.
- Context: they route outbound traffic through a corporate proxy; suspected buffering of small SSE chunks.
- Workaround: customer disabled proxy buffering (nginx proxy_buffering off) and added client read timeout + retry.
- 2026-02-14: Customer reports improvement; still sees rare stalls but acceptable for pilot; continuing evaluation.

source.next_step: AE to attempt 2nd outreach (call + hand-written note) week of 2026-02-10; pause sequence if no reply by 2026-02-24
timeline: 2025-11-03 - Lead created from webinar signup (marketing) - initial qual by SDR
2025-11-10 - Intro call (Jordan Blake + Priya K.) - high level requirements: reduce agent-handling time, better KB search
2025-12-12 - Security questionnaire sent (vendor-scan.pdf) - asked about SOC2 and KMS; response in progress
2026-01-15 - Technical scoping call - latency SLOs 100-200ms p95, throughput target 300 reqs/s peak
2026-01-22 - Live demo (hosted API) attended by CTO, Head of AI, 3 engineers; demo recording: fireflies_call_1b7c9a
2026-01-23 - Pricing deck + sample SLA sent (drive/fjordgate/pricing-v2.pdf)
2026-01-28 - AE follow-up email; CTO replied: 'tight budget next qtr, want to revisit in March'
2026-02-03 - Quick check-in voicemail left by Jordan - no response
2026-02-10 - Updated CRM: moved to nurture; AE to attempt one last reach before marking stalled
notes: Post-demo: CTO and Head of AI were positive on latency and batching demo; impressed with automatic prefix cache demo.
Quote (CTO): 'Love the runtime but we need firm answers on VPC onboarding timeline and audit log retention (3 years).'
Security flagged KMS integration and data residency as blockers for now — security team requested architecture diagram (shared, awaiting reply).
AE sent two tailored price scenarios (hosted with committed usage, vs dedicated entry-level) — CFO said Q1 budget freeze.
SE notes: model preference leaning open models + fallback to smaller quantized variant for cost-sensitive routes; asked about automatic fallback policy details.
Follow-up attempts: 3 emails, 1 voicemail, 1 LinkedIn InMail (no replies since 01-28).
Risk: internal champion (Head of AI) is being reassigned to backend infra in March — risk of losing momentum.
Suggested play: pause heavy sales cadence; nurture with technical content (VPC onboarding checklist, SOC2 evidence) and re-engage in March.
Associated artifacts: fireflies_call_1b7c9a (demo), gmail_thread_17b4c9f (pricing thread), drive/fjordgate/pricing-v2.pdf, jira:FG-SUP-77 (security questionnaire ticket).

source.next_step: Security + production readiness review: share telemetry/PII posture for onboarding events, confirm VPC deployment path and SLA options; schedule POC kickoff with their support-agent workflow.
blockers: Wants clearer go-live / production checklist for internal enablement + change management
Concerned about onboarding time (engineering time to first successful call + streaming reliability)
Needs confirmation on data residency (US-only) and retention defaults for logs/telemetry
Procurement requires SSO/SAML + audit logs confirmation before POC expansion
timeline: 2025-01-23 — Inbound via website ("support agent rollout Q1"), requested latency/cost guidance and a 'go-live checklist'.
2025-01-30 — Discovery call (Ben Carter + Jean-Paul Laurent). Current stack: mixed OpenAI + self-hosted embedding service; looking to consolidate inference + routing + better ops tooling.

2025-02-06 — Demo + onboarding friction discussion (Fireflies: 2025-02-06-northstar-support-onboarding-feedback). Key theme: slow onboarding + unclear prod steps; asked for in-console checklist and 'first production launch' guidance.
2025-02-12 — Follow-up email: shared Redwood Hosted API quickstart + streaming chat example; they reported first attempt hit auth/env var confusion ("we lost 30–45 min to 401s").
2025-02-20 — Tech deep dive (SE-led): streaming SSE behavior, retry patterns, and quota/rate-limit handling; discussed Dedicated vs Hosted for predictable throughput.
2025-03-06 — Internal note: prospect wants checklist prototype in Console; asked whether checklist progress is visible to admins/support for troubleshooting.
source.notes:
Account summary:
- Mid-market support platform building LLM-powered agent assist (draft replies + case summarization) embedded in their product.
- Primary pain is not model quality, it’s operational friction: "getting to first successful call is too easy to mess up" and "we need a clear go-live path for our team and customers".

What they asked for (verbatim / paraphrase):
- "We need a production readiness checklist we can follow and share internally".
- "We don’t want surprises with quotas/rate limits once we turn on traffic".
- "Streaming disconnects are confusing — our engineers aren’t sure what’s expected vs broken".
- "Give us the steps for go-live: keys, env vars, first request, streaming, retries, monitoring, budgets." (their words)

Current state / context:
- They run a multi-tenant SaaS; will start with internal dogfood then phased rollout to a subset of customers.
- Traffic profile: spiky during business hours; mixture of short requests (agent suggestions) + longer summaries.
- Wants guardrails: timeouts/retries defaults, clear 429 handling guidance, and a way to pin model versions before broad rollout.
- Strong preference for US-only processing and documented retention controls.

Workload + requirements (captured by SE):
- Use cases: (1) streaming chat-like agent assist UI, (2) ticket/case summarization, (3) semantic retrieval over KB articles + past tickets.
- Latency: target P95 < 1.5s for first token in agent assist; summaries can be slower but need predictable throughput.
- Reliability: needs recommended retry/backoff for 429/5xx; wants explicit streaming reconnect strategy.
- Cost controls: budgets/quotas + alerts; wants token/latency breakdown to attribute costs per tenant.
- Model ops: model pinning for stable behavior; fallback policy if capacity constrained ("degrade gracefully rather than fail").

Product interest:
- Starts with Hosted API for POC; evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput if agent-assist adoption ramps.
- Interested in Redwood Console observability + (eventually) Optimize recommendations.

Security/compliance:
- Needs SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration, and written statement of telemetry/event payload PII posture.
- Data residency: US-only; requests clarity on where onboarding/usage telemetry is stored + retention.

Open questions:
- Whether they require VPC in the first 90 days or can start Hosted API public and move to VPC later.
- Who owns rollout of the checklist experience in their org (platform team vs product engineers).

Internal actions:
- Use the new Console onboarding checklist as a selling point: show checklist steps + deep links + admin/support override concept.
- Provide 'first production launch' checklist doc + map each step to concrete how-to pages (API keys, streaming, retries/timeouts, quotas/budgets, usage dashboard, model pinning/fallback, observability hooks).

Call artifact:
- Fireflies transcript: 2025-02-06-northstar-support-onboarding-feedback (demo call focused on onboarding friction and checklist request).
source.requirements_summary: Northstar is evaluating Redwood to power an LLM-based support agent assist + summarization feature. Key decision criteria: (1) onboarding speed and clarity (TTFS/TTFP), (2) production-safe defaults for streaming + retries/timeouts, (3) predictable cost controls (quotas/budgets + usage analytics), (4) model pinning + fallback routing, and (5) security posture (SSO/SAML, audit logs, US data residency, retention controls, KMS). They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.
source.data_quality_flags: Domain unverified (from inbound form). Confirm HQ + legal entity name during next call.

They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.

Key decision criteria: (1) onboarding speed and clarity (TTFS/TTFP), (2) production-safe defaults for streaming + retries/timeouts, (3) predictable cost controls (quotas/budgets + usage analytics), (4) model pinning + fallback routing, and (5) security posture (SSO/SAML, audit logs, US data residency, retention controls, KMS). They prefer VPC deployment but may start on Hosted API if security review can proceed in parallel.

source.next_step: Re-engage via Director intro + customized security brief; propose 6-week POC credits and reduced pilot pricing
timeline: 2024-11-12 - Lead created from inbound. Initial product fit survey completed.
2024-11-20 - Discovery call (Jordan + Priya) - discussed clinical search + reranking for literature and notes ingestion. Customer: wants sub-second query latency for clinician-facing UI.
2024-12-04 - Security intro call with IT (requested HIPAA/KMS details). They asked for SOC2 + evidence of data residency options.
2025-01-15 - Demo attended (Product demo: embeddings + rerank + streaming chat). Live walkthrough of POC sketch. Fireflies: ff_20250219_kythera_demo (note: transcript uploaded).
2025-01-17 - Sent tailored pricing + POC outline + security FAQ (drive links). Asked for pilot approval from Legal and InfoSec.
2025-02-19 - Follow-up call scheduled; technical deep-dive offered (SE). Attended but low engagement near the end; asked for written answers to architecture & residency questions.
2025-02-25 - Last email from prospect: "We are in a hiring freeze and InfoSec is backlogged. Will circle back Q2 if priorities shift." No response after two nudges.
2025-03-04 - AE updated CRM to 'stalled' and flagged for Director escalation; propose exec intro and 6-week POC credits to revive.
source.notes:
Post-demo silence. Demo attended and product fit was positive (search + rerank + chat for clinicians). Key asks: HIPAA controls, KMS, on-prem option or VPC, data residency in us-east, tokenization/PII redaction. They explicitly asked for quantized model recommendations and cost estimate for 1000 qps inference (typical clinician load).

Current state: Legal/InfoSec placed hold — heavy backlog + demands for SOC2 report copies and encryption-at-rest details. Internal champion (Head of Data Science) changed teams last month -> no clear owner to push pilot. Budget conversation paused due to org-wide hiring/budget freeze through Q2.

Competitive pressure: they have an existing relationship with Azure and are evaluating Anthropic for safety features. Prospect comment on demo: "liked the observability and rollout features" but: "we need security sign-off before procurement."

Suggested approach: send tailored security packet (SOC2 + architecture + KMS playbook), offer 6-week POC credits + technical onboarding support, loop in Director for exec-to-exec nudging. Leave cadence: 1x/month nurture with value emails, re-open in 2025-06.
